senses.gloss: the first line of a qasida or ghazal

examples.english: It is all peace until the break of dawn.

senses.gloss: any place that celestial bodies rise.

examples.text: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطۡلِعَ ٱلشَّمۡسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطۡلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوۡمٖ لَّمۡ نَجۡعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتۡرٗا
examples.english: until he reached the rising ˹point˺ of the sun. He found it rising on a people for whom We had provided no shelter from it.

etymology.text:
Verbal noun and noun of place from the verb طَلَعَ (ṭalaʕa).

Derived from the active participle of اِطَّلَعَ (iṭṭalaʕa, “to take insight”).
